verdict.answer_first: A is closer: it opens with a concrete default path, 'recommended default path... short maintenance window... managed Postgres'; B opens with setup framing, 'No problem — I'll give you the plan,' before the actual prescription.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to one path: 'recommended default path' and 'maintenance window path'; B adds more branches and mandates, e.g. 'PgBouncer... from day one' plus 'logical replication / dual-write + CDC.'
verdict.fabrication: A invents context with 'You dismissed the questions'; both make unsupported rankings: A says 'the #1 source of migration breakage,' B says 'the #1 surprise.'
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it keeps the sequence tight: stand up PG, port schema, fix SQLite-isms, migrate, verify, cut over. B has useful details but adds staging, pooling, PITR, monitoring, and phases that dilute the immediate migration runbook.
